metadata.summary: Fetch regulatory approval data for drug terms

documentation:
# 🤖 regbot 🤖

[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/regbot.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/regbot)
[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/regbot.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/regbot)
[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/regbot.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/regbot)
[![Actions status](https://github.com/genomicmedlab/regbot/actions/workflows/checks.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/genomicmedlab/regbot/actions/checks.yaml)

<!-- description -->
Fetch regulatory approval data for drug terms
<!-- /description -->



---

## Installation

Install from [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/regbot/):

```shell
python3 -m pip install regbot
```

---

## Development

Clone the repo and create a virtual environment:

```shell
git clone https://github.com/genomicmedlab/regbot
cd regbot
python3 -m virtualenv venv
source venv/bin/activate
```

Install development dependencies and `pre-commit`:

```shell
python3 -m pip install -e '.[dev,tests]'
pre-commit install
```

Check style with `ruff`:

```shell
python3 -m ruff format . && python3 -m ruff check --fix .
```

Run tests with `pytest`:

```shell
pytest
```

modules.name: regbot
documentation: Fetch regulatory approval data for drug terms

modules.name: regbot.fetch.class_utils
documentation: Provide helper methods for imposing structure onto raw data fetched from external APIs.

functions.name: regbot.fetch.class_utils.map_to_enum
documentation: Use in enum _missing_ methods to map alternate constructions to python-legal values.

modules.name: regbot.fetch
documentation: Fetch data from regulatory agency APIs.

modules.name: regbot.fetch.rxclass
documentation: Fetch data from RxClass API.

functions.name: regbot.fetch.rxclass.make_rxclass_request
documentation:
Issue an API request to RxClass.

```
param url

RxClass API URL to request

param include_snomedct

if True, include class claims provided by SNOMEDCT.
These are provided under a different license from the rest of the data and
may present publishability issues for data consumers.

param normalize

if True, try to normalize values to controlled enumerations
and appropriate Python datatypes

return

processed list of drug class descriptions from RxClass
```

functions.name: regbot.fetch.rxclass.get_drug_class_info
documentation:
Get RxClass-provided drug info.

See also RxClass getClassByRxNormDrugName API:

: <https://lhncbc.nlm.nih.gov/RxNav/APIs/api-RxClass.getClassByRxNormDrugName.html>

```
param drug

RxNorm-provided drug name

param include_snomedct

if True, include class claims provided by SNOMEDCT.
These are provided under a different license from the rest of the data and
may present publishability issues for data consumers.

param normalize

if True, try to normalize values to controlled enumerations
and appropriate Python datatypes

return

list of drug class descriptions from RxClass
```

See https://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm/docs/appendix5.html

documentation:

classes.name: regbot.fetch.rxclass.RelationSource
documentation: Constrain relation source values.

classes.name: regbot.fetch.rxclass.Relation
documentation: Constrain relation values.

modules.name: regbot.fetch.drugsfda
documentation: Provide utilities for interacting with Drugs@FDA API endpoint.

functions.name: regbot.fetch.drugsfda.make_drugsatfda_request
documentation:
Get <mailto:Drugs@FDA> data given an API query URL.

```
param url

URL to request

param normalize

if True, try to normalize values to controlled enumerations
and appropriate Python datatypes

param limit

# of results per page

return

list of Drugs@FDA ``Result``s if successful

source:7: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.

raise RequestException

if HTTP response status != 200
```

functions.name: regbot.fetch.drugsfda.get_anda_results
documentation:
Get <mailto:Drugs@FDA> data for an ANDA ID.

```
param anda

ANDA code (should be a six-digit number formatted as a string)

param normalize

if True, try to normalize values to controlled enumerations
and appropriate Python datatypes

return

list of Drugs@FDA ``Result``s if successful

source:6: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.
```

functions.name: regbot.fetch.drugsfda.get_nda_results
documentation:
Get <mailto:Drugs@FDA> data for an NDA ID.

```
param nda

NDA code (should be a six-digit number formatted as a string)

param normalize

if True, try to normalize values to controlled enumerations
and appropriate Python datatypes

return

list of Drugs@FDA ``Result``s if successful

source:6: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.
```

classes.name: regbot.fetch.drugsfda.ProductMarketingStatus
documentation:
'Marketing status indicates how a drug product is sold in the United States. Drug
products in Drugs@FDA are identified as:

* Prescription
* Over-the-counter
* Discontinued
* None - drug products that have been tentatively approved'

https://www.fda.gov/drugs/drug-approvals-and-databases/drugsfda-glossary-terms#marketing_status

Provide values for product routes.

'A dosage form is the physical form in which a drug is produced and dispensed,
such as a tablet, a capsule, or an injectable.'

modules.name: regbot.fetch.clinical_trials
documentation:
Fetch data from FDA Clinical Trials API.

See
\* API description: <https://clinicaltrials.gov/data-api/api>
\* Bioregistry entry: <https://bioregistry.io/registry/clinicaltrials>

..code-block:: pycon

> ```pycon
> >>> results = get_clinical_trials("imatinib")
> >>> results[0].protocol.identification.nct_id
> 'clinicaltrials:NCT00769782'
> >>> len(results)
> 755
> ```

Filter studies by age criteria:

..code-block:: pycon

> ```pycon
> >>> import datetime
> >>> ped_age = datetime.timedelta(365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 18)
> >>> pediatric_studies = [
> ...     r
> ...     for r in results
> ...     if r.protocol.eligibility
> ...     and r.protocol.eligibility.max_age
> ...     and r.protocol.eligibility.max_age < ped_age
> ... ]
> ```

Get all serious adverse events:

```pycon
>>> serious_events = []
>>> for r in results:
>>>     if f.results and r.results.adverse_events and r.results.adverse_events.serious_events:
>>>          serious_events.append(event.term)
>>> serious_events[:3]
['ANAEMIA', 'AUTOIMMUNE HAEMOLYTIC ANAEMIA', 'EVANS SYNDROME']
```

```
param url

URL to request. This method doesn't add any additional parameters except
for pagination.

return

studies contained in API response
```

functions.name: regbot.fetch.clinical_trials.get_clinical_trials
documentation:
Get data from the FDA Clinical Trials API.

```pycon
>>> results = get_clinical_trials("imatinib")
>>> results[0].protocol.identification.nct_id
'NCT00769782'
```

```
param drug_name

name of drug used for trial intervention. This is passed to the
API intervention parameter, which appears to search for inclusion as a substring
rather than a full-span match

return

list of matching trial descriptions
```
